task: Implement a JavaScript function `lowerBound(arr, target)` that returns the leftmost index at which `target` can be inserted into the already-sorted (ascending) array `arr` so the array stays sorted. Equivalently, return the first index `i` where `arr[i] >= target`; if every element is strictly less than `target`, return `arr.length`. The array may contain duplicates: when `target` is present, return the index of its FIRST (leftmost) occurrence, not any later one. Use a binary search; do not scan linearly. Watch the search-bound and midpoint arithmetic carefully — the common off-by-one traps are an inclusive vs half-open upper bound and collapsing the window on the wrong side of an equal element.
visibleSpec: lowerBound(arr, target) -> integer index in [0, arr.length]. arr is sorted ascending and may contain duplicate values. Result is the leftmost insertion point keeping arr sorted; for a value already present it is the index of the first equal element.
scope: Single pure function. No mutation of arr, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O.
